Title: The glutathione transferase of Nicotiana benthamiana NbGSTU4 plays a role in regulating the early replication of Bamboo mosaic virus

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cellular redox buffering and antioxidation; supports a reduced environment favorable for Bamboo mosaic virus minus-strand RNA synthesis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: upregulated after Bamboo mosaic virus infection
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: upregulated after Bamboo mosaic virus infection
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: TRV-based virus-induced gene silencing; transient overexpression; mutant analysis of GSH-binding site (S13A); UV-crosslinking RNA-binding assay; in vitro RdRp assay; qRT-PCR/Northern blot/Western blot
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes Bamboo mosaic virus RNA accumulation by binding the viral 3' UTR and facilitating GSH-dependent early minus-strand RNA synthesis; knockdown decreases viral RNA accumulation and overexpression increases it
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts as a host factor for Potexvirus infection; knockdown reduced BaMV and PVX accumulation but not CMV or TMV; RNA binding to BaMV 3' UTR is enhanced by GSH and is specific over the minus-strand 3' terminal promoter; S13A mutation abolishing GSH-binding activity eliminates enhancement of BaMV accumulation
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential host target for engineering resistance to Bamboo mosaic virus or related potexviruses by reducing NbGSTU4 function/expression; caution that the paper studies viral replication rather than nutritional trait improvement
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter used for infectious BaMV cDNA clone pKB; transient expression constructs for NbGSTU4-GFP/NbGSTU4-S13A-GFP promoter not clearly stated
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious morphological changes in NbGSTU4-knockdown plants were observed under the tested conditions
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbGSTU4 is a BaMV-induced tau-class GST in Nicotiana benthamiana. 2. Silencing NbGSTU4 reduced BaMV plus- and minus-strand RNA accumulation, while transient expression increased viral coat protein accumulation. 3. Recombinant NbGSTU4 binds the BaMV 3' UTR in a GSH-enhanced manner and supports a reduced environment for early minus-strand RNA synthesis.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: BaMV infection; GSH treatment; reducing versus oxidizing conditions in RdRp assay
